task_completion.description: Verify that the AI segmentation file has been loaded into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ai_segmentation' or 'ai_segmentation.nii.gz' listed in the Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or Segment Editor list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the reference (ground truth) segmentation file has been loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'BraTS2021_00000_seg' or 'BraTS2021_00000_seg.nii.gz' listed in the Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or Segment Editor list.

task_completion.description: Verify that the user has navigated to the Segment Comparison module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel title at the top of the left sidebar should read 'Segment Comparison'.

task_completion.description: Verify that segmentation metrics (Dice and Hausdorff) have been computed and are displayed in the UI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a results table within the Segment Comparison module displaying 'Dice' (value approx 0.75-0.85) and 'Hausdorff' (value approx 5-30).

task_completion.description: Verify that the results have been exported to a JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor window showing JSON content, a file browser showing 'segmentation_metrics.json', or the Python console executing code to save the file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the 3D Slicer application window with the Segment Comparison module, not just a terminal script execution.

integrity.description: Verify that the exported values match the computed values in the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the JSON file content is visible, the Dice and Hausdorff values should match those shown in the Slicer results table.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not just copy the expected ranges from the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The result should be a specific number (e.g., 0.782), not a range (e.g., '0.75-0.85').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The AI segmentation filename is 'ai_segmentation.nii.gz'. The reference filename is 'BraTS2021_00000_seg.nii.gz'. Expected Dice coefficient is approximately 0.75 to 0.85. Expected Hausdorff distance is approximately 5 to 30 mm.
